_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**1. Facility Open Burning Compliance Controls (Immediate — in force May 1)**
The workspace must build or confirm an operational control prohibiting open burning at all Georgia facilities, logistics yards, and staging areas located within the 54-county ban zone. Scope includes: permanent warehouse locations, temporary event staging sites, film & TV production support lots, and automotive storage yards. Required system elements:
- A written facility-level policy prohibiting burning of yard debris, land-clearing waste, storm debris, and weed abatement materials during May 1 – September 30
- Subcontractor/vendor contract clauses requiring burn-ban compliance and indemnifying the workspace for third-party violations at shared sites
- Identification of alternative debris disposal routes (commercial grinding/composting operations or C&D landfills) for each Georgia facility
- Designation of an EPD District Office contact number for each facility's county (Mountain District: 404-362-2671; Northeast: 706-369-6376; East Central: 706-667-4343; West Central: 478-751-6612)

Gap from baseline: Most freight forwarders operating in Georgia do not maintain seasonal environmental calendars; a recurring May 1 compliance activation and September 30 deactivation workflow should be embedded in the facility operations calendar.

**2. Drought Condition Monitoring Protocol (Active — April 27, 2026 onwards)**
The workspace must implement a lightweight monitoring protocol for the EPD Drought Response level, given the potential for escalation from Level 1 (no mandatory restrictions) to Level 2 (outdoor water use limits) or Level 3 (further restrictions). Scope: all Georgia facilities on public water supply. Required elements:
- Weekly check of the EPD Drought Management dashboard (epd.georgia.gov/watershed-protection-branch/drought-management) assigned to a named compliance contact
- A pre-drafted facility water-use advisory to be activated if drought escalates to Level 2, covering landscape irrigation scheduling (currently permissible only 4 PM – 10 AM under Level 1), cooling systems, and washing operations
- Tracking of U.S. Drought Monitor classifications for Georgia counties where facilities are located

Gap from baseline: EPD considers precipitation, stream flows, groundwater, reservoir levels, short-term climate predictions, and soil moisture in determining escalation; these are not factors workspaces traditionally track, so a delegated monitoring assignment is the minimum viable control.

**3. NPDES Construction Stormwater GEOS Registration (Pre-construction — rolling)**
For any planned Georgia facility construction, expansion, or logistics hub buildout disturbing one or more acres, the workspace must complete GEOS registration and NOI submission prior to ground-breaking. Required operational elements:
- GEOS account registration (epd.georgia.gov/geos/) with a designated Responsible Official (RO)
- SWPPP prepared and on-site before disturbance begins
- Quarterly visual inspections of all discharge points documented in the SWPPP
- Electronic NOT submission upon project completion

Gap from baseline: The 2023 NPDES permits require electronic submittals only; any legacy paper-based permit management processes are non-compliant. Current permits run through July 31, 2028.

*Source: Georgia EPD Summer Open Burning Ban page. https://epd.georgia.gov/air-protection-branch/open-burning-rules-georgia/summer-open-burning-ban; Drought Management, Georgia EPD. https://epd.georgia.gov/watershed-protection-branch/drought-management; NPDES Construction Stormwater General Permits, Georgia EPD. https://epd.georgia.gov/forms-permits/watershed-protection-branch-forms-permits/storm-water-forms/npdes-construction.*
